source.title: Bridging sparsity to cost: annotated brief and experiment plan
source.content:
Quick purpose: sketch and annotate a short reading + experiment plan that connects recent sparsity/NLP papers to concrete inference cost levers we can try in Redwood. Not a literature review — targeted notes aimed at an engineering sprint (latency/cost first, accuracy second).

High-level thinking (one-liners):
- Sparsity is not a single knob: structured (block/row) vs unstructured pruning vs conditional compute (MoE / routing) have very different engineering and latency implications.
- For serving economics, effective BOPs/token and memory traces matter more than just FLOPs in isolation. Implementation complexity and kernel availability (cuSparse, Triton) decide real cost.
- Weak hypothesis: small/moderate structured sparsity + aggressive quantization yields the best cost-quality tradeoff for 8–32k context models on GPU inference without re-architecting serving stack. Conditional compute shines for throughput-heavy async workloads.

Annotated paper notes (short):
1) "Sparse Transformer variants" (paper A - arXiv 2021-ish) — takeaways:
   - Core idea: replace dense attention with sparse patterns (local + strided + random).
   - Practical note: pattern compliance simplifies kernels; fixed patterns map well to CUDA kernels and reduce memory bandwidth.
   - Experiment idea: implement a patterned attention shim for a small decoder-only model (1B) and measure end-to-end latency on T4/A10 for short vs long prompts.
   - Risks: pattern may harm retrieval-style prompts; need targeted eval sets.

2) "Magnitude pruning and gradual unstructured sparsity" (paper B) — takeaways:
   - Core idea: iterative magnitude pruning with warm restarts produces high sparsity with limited quality loss if fine-tuned.
   - Engineering: unstructured sparsity yields sparse matrices that are currently hard to accelerate on GPUs for inference except via sparse-dense kernels or specialized hardware.
   - Back-of-envelope: 80% unstructured sparsity could reduce memory footprint but may not reduce runtime token cost on standard GPUs.
   - Decision: deprioritize unstructured pruning for hosted path unless we target CPU / sparse accelerators.

3) "Mixture of Experts (MoE) and routing for inference" (paper C) — takeaways:
   - Core idea: conditional routing to experts reduces per-token compute by activating a subset of weights.
   - Serving considerations: routing overhead, memory sharding of experts, cross-device communication. Good for throughput and batchy workloads but adds tail latencies for small-batch interactive queries.
   - Experiment idea: synthetic MoE simulation: emulate 2-expert activation pattern by replacing some FFN layers with low-cost adapter blocks and measure token-level latency variance.

4) "Structured block sparsity + hardware kernels" (paper D / sys paper) — takeaways:
   - Block sparsity (4x4, 8x8) is the pragmatic sweet spot: allows blocked GEMM speedups and simpler memory layout; good for Triton/TVM autotuning.
   - Action: test 8x8 block pruning on a 2B model export and try cuBLAS/cuSparse-backed blocked matmul on A10.

Practical experiments (prioritized):
- EXP-01: 8x8 Block pruning pipeline (no fine-tune)
  - Steps: prune weights using magnitude block criterion -> export quantized FP16/INT8 model -> run end-to-end bench harness (latency P50/P95, throughput)
  - Metrics: tokens/sec, p50 latency, p95 latency, output LM perplexity on held-out eval set, hallucination indicator (QA exact match drop).
  - Success criteria: >=15% decrease in cost/token for <=2% perplexity increase.

- EXP-02: Patterned attention shim (local+stride)
  - Steps: instrument attention code path for configurable pattern, run microbench kernels vs baseline dense attention for seq-len 512, 2048, 8192.
  - Metrics: kernel time, memory BW, end-to-end latency on sample prompt mix.
  - Dependencies: Triton kernel tweak or rely on fused C++ attention in serving runtime.

- EXP-03: MoE emulation for small-batch interactive queries
  - Steps: create adapter-based conditional compute in FFN layers that emulate expert gating; measure variance in response times and median cost/token.
  - Motivation: validate whether conditional compute helps interactive tail cost without major infra change.

Evaluation sets (initial):
- Latency-focused: 5000 short prompts (1-3 turns), 3000 medium (200-1000 tokens), 200 long-window tasks (2–8k) sampled from internal user anonymized trace (with product team OK).
- Quality-focused: small curated suite of QA + summarization + code generation prompts to detect culturally harmful regressions.

Quick sketches / notes-to-self: 
- Kernel availability: check A10 vs H100 differences for blocked matmul GPU speedups; add short matrix of perf target numbers.
- Quantization lens: pair each sparsity run with PTQ INT8 and GPTQ variants; hybrid approach might be key.
- Caching interaction: sparser models may have different KV-cache memory characteristics; run KV cache memory usage checks.
- Ops we can try without training: structured block prune -> linear re-ordering -> little/no fine-tune (cheap path).

Open questions / unknowns: 
- What sparsity patterns preserve softmax gradients used in RLHF? (important if we plan to fine-tune later).
- Are there existing Triton kernels in our repo that can be adapted quickly for blocked GEMM? (ask infra).
- Cross-product risk: block-sparsity + aggressive quant -> catastrophic calibration drift for logit scaling? design calibration checks.

Next steps (concrete):
- (Today) Draft PR template for EXP-01 with perf harness checklist (owner: Mateo).
- Book 90m spike session with infra + serving runtime next week to validate kernel feasibility (invite: infra, srt, applied-ml).
- Run quick probe: export 1B FP16 model, apply 8x8 block prune at 30%, 50%, 70% sparsity and measure model load & a single token step time on A10 (owner: me).
- Add small eval runner to compare outputs (diff-based) and record examples where outputs diverge; collect human-readable examples for product.

Notes on reproducibility and logging: 
- For each experiment tag the model export with git sha + prune seed + prune round -> store in S3 path: s3://redwood-experiments/sparsity/{date}/{tag}.
- Add per-run trace: kernel-level timing, memory snapshots, nvprof traces for interesting points.

Random thoughts (brain-dump):
- Could we build a lightweight "sparsity simulator" that estimates GPU wall-time from FLOPs + memory bandwidth + kernel-specific speedups? Even a heuristic would help prioritize which sparsity pattern to pursue.
- Partner with partners team to see if any customers want to co-sponsor a MoE POC (they care about throughput for batch ingestion).

TODOs and small tickets:
- TODO-1: create experiment stub: GH issue / template -> link when created.
- TODO-2: ping infra about blocked GEMM kernels availability and A10 perf matrix.
- TODO-3: select 200 representative user prompts for latency bench -> product privacy review.

References & quick links:
- Patterned attention survey (arXiv link above)
- MoE routing notes (internal Confluence: CONFLUENCE-INTERNAL-MOE-ROUTING)
- cuSparse / Triton performance readouts (internal GH issue linked above)

End-of-doc: keep this short and iterated. Planning to convert into a working doc with step-by-step experiment checklist once infra confirms kernel availability.
source.summary: Short annotated notes tying sparsity approaches (block, pattern, MoE, unstructured) to practical inference experiments prioritized for cost and latency. Includes experiment plan for block pruning and patterned attention, evaluation sets, open questions, and concrete next steps.

source.title: 1:1 playbook — Aisha (manager) / Ryan (report) — May 12 2026
source.content:
Context / purpose:
- Quick prep notes before 1:1 with Ryan (IC on serving-runtime). Short (30m). Goal: unblock immediate work, calibrate priorities, and check on career / motivation.

Status snapshot (what I know / want confirmed):
- On-call last week: pager spikes on node boot for quantized models — Ryan owned the postmortem doc but said he still needs time to propose a mitigation. Follow up here.
- PR 412 (runtime): Ryan is the primary author; tests flapping in CI intermittently. He mentioned a flaky integration test that only shows up under heavy batching.
- OKR alignment: Ryan listed a goal to reduce 95th latency for small-seq gen by 10% this quarter; current dashboard shows ~6% improvement so far.
- Career: Ryan expressed interest in mentoring and leading small projects; asked about promotion expectations back in April.

Primary agenda (30 minutes):
1) Fast unblock (10m)
   - Quick status on PR 412: what is failing now? What help do you need (test infra, a runbook, more eyes)?
   - Postmortem status for on-call incident: have we captured root cause and immediate mitigation? Any temporary config we should roll out?
2) Priorities & scope (8m)
   - Which work should land this sprint vs be deferred? (latency optimizations vs flaky-test cleanup)
   - Tradeoffs: if we prioritize latency improvements, what's the risk to stability?
3) Career sync (7m)
   - Intent: understand what 'lead small projects' means to Ryan; concrete examples he can take in next 6–8 weeks.
   - Promotion checklist: current gaps (ownership, design docs, cross-team influence) — confirm if he agrees and wants to target a promotion cycle in Q3.
4) Quick coaching + wrap (5m)
   - One piece of feedback I want to give: better PR descriptions (repro steps for flaky tests).
   - Ask Ryan for one ask of me (what can I remove/block for you this week?).

Questions / prompts for Ryan (notes-to-self so I actually ask them):
- "Walk me through the failing test: steps to reproduce locally? How often does it break in CI?"
- "If you had one extra day this sprint, where would you invest it and why?"
- "Who on infra or SRE would we loop in to investigate the node boot time?"
- "What are two small ownership projects you could run end-to-end in 6 weeks?"

Signals to watch during the conversation:
- If Ryan is vague about mitigation plans => offer to pair with Ben for a focused bug-squash session.
- If Ryan says he feels burned out => scale back non-critical work, check load, offer time off.
- If Ryan is strongly motivated about mentoring => propose concrete mentoring slot (1–2 hrs/month) and pair him with intern.

Draft feedback (keep it actionable):
- Positive: Great debugging persistence on the quantized benchmark logs; your triage notes were very readable.
- Improve: Add a short repro section to PR descriptions and tag related flaky tests (LIN-3456). For critical flakes, include a small test plan to reduce CI noise.

Action items (capture during or immediately after meeting):
- "Ryan — add a repro snippet and expected flake behavior to PR 412 by 2026-05-14. (helps triage)"
- "Aisha — schedule 1h pairing with Ben this week to reproduce CI flaky test (target 2026-05-13)."
- "Ryan — draft two 6-week project proposals for mentoring/leadership (one infra-focused, one feature-focused) by 2026-05-21; add to Confluence page."
- "Aisha — escalate node-boot spike to SRE/infra with data snapshot and proposal for temporary throttle (by 2026-05-14)."
- "Ryan — update on-call postmortem doc with proposed mitigation and rollback plan by 2026-05-15 (link back to LIN-3456)."

Notes-to-self:
- Bring up promotion rubric only if Ryan signals readiness; don't push calendar. If he wants, map gaps to specific actions and sponsor him to PLG review in Q3 cycle.
- Offer to remove one meeting from his calendar for the next 2 weeks to free focused time.

Misc quick links (to paste in chat during 1:1):
- PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/412
- Linear: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/LIN-3456
- Confluence: https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/ryan-growth-plan
- Slack thread: https://redwood.slack.com/archives/C02ENG12345/p1680000000000

Post-meeting (how I want to follow up):
- Send short recap email (1–2 bullets + action owners) within 24 hours.
- Add check-in reminder for action items in my calendar for the due dates.
- Revisit promotion conversation only after he completes two small leadership projects.

Tone & personal reminders:
- Keep the conversation lightweight and direct; Ryan responds well to concrete asks and bounded time.
- Balance technical troubleshooting with coaching — ensure at least one concrete career ask is captured.
action_items: Ryan - add repro snippet + expected flake behavior to PR 412 by 2026-05-14
Aisha - schedule 1h pairing with Ben to reproduce CI flaky test by 2026-05-13
Ryan - draft two 6-week project proposals and add to Confluence by 2026-05-21
Aisha - escalate node-boot spike to SRE/infra with data snapshot by 2026-05-14
Ryan - update on-call postmortem with mitigation & rollback plan by 2026-05-15

- Add check-in reminder for action items in my calendar for the due dates.
- Revisit promotion conversation only after he completes two small leadership projects.

Tone & personal reminders:
- Keep the conversation lightweight and direct; Ryan responds well to concrete asks and bounded time.
- Balance technical troubleshooting with coaching — ensure at least one concrete career ask is captured.

- Quick prep notes before 1:1 with Ryan (IC on serving-runtime). Short (30m). Goal: unblock immediate work, calibrate priorities, and check on career / motivation.

Status snapshot (what I know / want confirmed):
- On-call last week: pager spikes on node boot for quantized models — Ryan owned the postmortem doc but said he still needs time to propose a mitigation. Follow up here.
- PR 412 (runtime): Ryan is the primary author; tests flapping in CI intermittently. He mentioned a flaky integration test that only shows up under heavy batching.
- OKR alignment: Ryan listed a goal to reduce 95th latency for small-seq gen by 10% this quarter; current dashboard shows ~6% improvement so far.
- Career: Ryan expressed interest in mentoring and leading small projects; asked about promotion expectations back in April.

Primary agenda (30 minutes):
1) Fast unblock (10m)
   - Quick status on PR 412: what is failing now? What help do you need (test infra, a runbook, more eyes)?
   - Postmortem status for on-call incident: have we captured root cause and immediate mitigation? Any temporary config we should roll out?
2) Priorities & scope (8m)
   - Which work should land this sprint vs be deferred? (latency optimizations vs flaky-test cleanup)
   - Tradeoffs: if we prioritize latency improvements, what's the risk to stability?
3) Career sync (7m)

4) Quick coaching + wrap (5m)

Questions / prompts for Ryan (notes-to-self so I actually ask them):
- "Walk me through the failing test: steps to reproduce locally? How often does it break in CI?"
- "If you had one extra day this sprint, where would you invest it and why?"
- "Who on infra or SRE would we loop in to investigate the node boot time?"
- "What are two small ownership projects you could run end-to-end in 6 weeks?"

- If Ryan says he feels burned out => scale back non-critical work, check load, offer time off.

source.title: Bridge sync prep: product + eng + applied-ml (scratch)
source.content:
Context:
- Quick personal scratchpad for the 60m bridge sync between Product (Tess), Serving Runtime (Marco), and Applied ML (Arjun).
- Goal: align on the next two-quarter priorities where product wants a single predictable latency target for hosted API endpoints while Applied ML wants to push a quantized 8-bit variant for certain families. Engineering needs clarity on rollout constraints and perf regression gates.
- Current tension: product target = p95 < 120ms for 4k tokens; ML wants to enable 8-bit at model-level to reduce cost but we’ve seen variable latency spikes in canaries.

Meeting goals / success criteria (what I want to walk out with):
1) Agreement on one prioritized experiment to validate quantized variants under realistic traffic (who owns, how long, success metric).
2) A short list of 3 gating metrics (latency p95, cost per 1k tokens, decoding failures) and thresholds for canary -> rollout.
3) Decision on whether we add a temporary routing policy to prefer unquantized on low-latency customers for 6 weeks.
4) Clear action owners and dates.

Proposed agenda (60m):
- 0-5m: Quick context & scoped ask (me)
- 5-20m: ML: summary of quantized candidate performance and open questions (Arjun)
- 20-35m: Eng: runtime observations from canary runs + KV/cache interaction notes (Marco)
- 35-50m: Product: customer impact, SLAs and risk appetite (Tess)
- 50-60m: Decide next steps + assign actions (all)

Background notes / recent signals:
- Canary traffic (week of 3/16) showed 8-bit variant reduced token cost by ~35% but p95 latency had a bimodal distribution — two clusters: baseline ~95ms and spikes to 220–300ms for sequences with heavy KV hits.
- KV cache misses correlate with a higher variance in attention kernel scheduling; runtime issue tracked in INF-712.
- User-facing SLA ask: Product has one pilot customer (FinApps) that requires <150ms p95. They are high volume and revenue-sensitive.

Open questions to raise:
- Can applied-ml produce a quantized build with deterministic memory layout to reduce GC/fragmentation effects? (Arjun)
- Is there a cheap routing policy we can toggle (region/model-variant preference) that avoids complex infra changes? (Marco)
- What guardrails do we need for failure modes (decode truncation, hallucination increase) — what evals do we run? (Tess)

Suggested gating metrics (draft, pick 3):
- Latency p95 (end-to-end server-side) for production-like inputs — threshold: <= 150ms for pilot customers.
- Token error rate / decode failures — threshold: <0.5% increased over baseline.
- Cost per 1k tokens (inferred from billing metrics) — expect >= 25% reduction for quantized variant to be worth tradeoffs.

Risks and mitigations (brief):
- Risk: spikey p95 during rare cache-thrashing patterns -> Mitigation: short-circuit routing to unquantized if p95 > threshold over sliding 10-minute window.
- Risk: subtle quality regressions not caught by standard evals -> Mitigation: add lightweight prompt set with known guardrail queries + manual review for first 48h after rollout.
- Risk: rollout orchestration complexity -> Mitigation: limit to single region and known customers (FinApps), use canary weights and automatic rollback if metric breach.

Notes I want from others (prep asks):
- Arjun: quick one-slide summary of quantization changes, top-3 known quality regressions, and an estimate for a determinism patch ETA.
- Marco: short list of runtime knobs we can flip remotely (routing, batch sizing, cache TTLs) and cost to implement a short-circuit routing policy.
- Tess: clarification on pilot customer SLA flex — how much wiggle room (15%? 25%?) and willingness to accept temporary routing exceptions.

Decision options (for quick voting during meeting):
A) Proceed with broadened canary (2 regions) and gating metrics above.
B) Limit experiment to pilot customer + single region, implement routing short-circuit for other customers.
C) Pause quantized rollout until deterministic memory layout fix merged and tested.

Action items (initial — assign/update in meeting):
- Arjun: prepare quantization one-pager + top-5 prompt eval set by 2026-04-06.
- Marco: prototype short-circuit routing toggle (design doc + rough effort estimate) by 2026-04-03; include rollback behavior.
- Tess: confirm pilot SLA flexibility with FinApps and draft customer comms template by 2026-04-05.
- Priya (me): schedule a 30m follow-up to review canary dashboards and runbook (target 2026-04-14).

Sticky notes for the meeting (things to say):
- "We should treat this as an economics + quality tradeoff; metrics must be simple and automated."
- "If we can't define an automatic short-circuit rollback, we should not widen the canary beyond pilot."

Quick metrics snippet to show live if asked:
- Baseline hosted p95 (last 7d): 112ms; 8-bit canary p95: median 98ms but 98th percentile spikes to 260ms (see INF-712).
- Cost delta observed in canary: -33% token cost (preliminary).

Slack / links to call out in meeting:
- Slack thread: #eng-runtime (link to thread: /p/caching-suggests)
- Confluence draft: /spaces/INF/pages/123456789/serving-runtime-roadmap (linked above)
- Runtime PR for 8-bit scheduling tweak: https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/482

After-meeting follow-up template (I'll send):
- Short summary of decisions, top 3 metrics and thresholds, owners + due dates, and next check-in date.

Misc scratch (do not present unless asked):
- Could we simulate a cached-heavy traffic pattern with synthetic workloads from perf infra? Marco might have a harness. If yes, run overnight to reproduce spikes.
- Consider a temporary per-customer model profile that pins FinApps to unquantized for 6 weeks and collects diagnostic traces for 2 weeks.

---
End of scratch — keep this informal. Will clean up after the meeting and post a one-paragraph summary in Confluence and the Slack thread.

source.title: Morning SDK todo scrapbook
source.content:
2026-03-13 morning scratchpad - devx SDK docs

Top-of-mind TODOs (quick wins first)
- [ ] add cURL streaming example to quickstart (users keep hitting blocking example)
  - short snippet for SSE + chunked transfer + abort controller note
  - link to streaming client impl: github PR 482 (TODO: reference exact file)
- [ ] fix python quickstart env var naming: currently uses API_KEY but examples and SDK expect REDWOOD_API_KEY -> inconsistency
- [ ] sample app gaps: minimal chat-react sample missing function-calling flow (how to wire client-side tools -> backend)
- [ ] add note on embeddings batching best-practices for front-end upload flow (chunk size suggestion, retries)
- [ ] tutorial: on-prem install checklist needs a KMS section (customers asking)

Short notes / scratch ideas
- Observed: folks copy-paste node example and forget to await stream -> leads to resource leak; add explicit await + error handler
- Consider small "Common gotchas" section inside each quickstart: token limits, streaming pitfalls, retry semantics, status codes mapping to SDK errors
- Add a small matrix: example language (py/node/rs) x feature (streaming, embeddings, function-calling, batching) -> which sample covers it? customers ask for one-liners

Mini schedule for today (estimates)
- 09:30-10:00 triage github issue backlog (label: docs:bug) -> close / assign (30m)
- 10:00-11:30 implement cURL + node streaming snippets (90m)
- 11:30-12:00 quick PR to fix env var naming in python quickstart (30m)
- 13:00-14:00 pair with Lina on chat-react function-calling flow (60m)
- 14:30-15:00 write short note for on-prem KMS checklist (30m)

Example snippets to include in doc (very rough)
- cURL streaming (SSE-like):
  curl -N -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDWOOD_API_KEY" \\
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \\
    -d '{"model":"gpt-4-x","input":"hello","stream":true}' \\
    https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate
  // note: use -N to disable buffering; mention platform proxies that chunk

- node streaming pattern (pseudo):
  const resp = await client.generate({ model: 'gpt-4-x', stream: true, input: 'hi' })
  for await (const part of resp.stream()) {
    // update UI, handle ctrl-c abort
  }

Open questions / followups
- Who owns the embeddings batching doc? Pinged @Ethan but not sure if it's product or infra recommended defaults. Agree on: 8-16 items / batch for CPU-bound preproc? check benchmarks
- Should we add a small "local dev mode" snippet for Redwood Private (VPC) so on-prem customers can run against local runtime mock? Good for offline demos
- Need to add a clear line about rate limit / quota errors: do we prefer 429 -> SDK throws RateLimitError? Confirm with runtime team (ticket: RI-2107)

Notes from yesterday sync with Platform (5m takeaways)
- Platform: new fallback routing semantics may return a compatible quantized variant -> docs need to show how to detect and surface to users
- Platform: telemetry will expose model_variant field in responses next week -> add example table to console docs

Small editorial tasks (10-20m each)
- replace screenshot in quickstart that shows old console UI
- update badge examples (npm install redwood-sdk -> note: new package name redwood-inference-sdk?) -> confirm package name
- add anchor links to "streaming" section for easier linking from support threads

Jira / GH quick links to create PRs for:
- RI-1234 (python quickstart env var) -> make PR from branch docs/quickstart-python-env-fix
- create new issue: docs: add streaming curl example -> link to PR after changes
- GH PR 482: review for streaming sample improvements (leave comment about await usage)

Personal reminders (notes to self)
- Leave 15m at EOD to write short changelog entry for docs changes (so release notes pick it up)
- Follow up with SRE on proxy buffering report (they reproduced chunking issue behind LB)

Draft blurb for PRs (copy into PR description)
- "This patch adds a non-blocking streaming example to the quickstart (cURL + Node) and clarifies the expected environment variable naming for the Python SDK. Also adds a short 'common gotchas' subsection for streaming clients. Tested locally against staging runtime."

Random quick links I keep pasting into bugs
- Slack: https://redwood.slack.com/archives/C01DEVXDOCS
- GH: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-sdk
- Confluence quickstart: https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DEV/pages/7890123/SDK+Docs

End of morning notes - keep this rough. Will tidy into PR description & small docs changes after pair session.

---
small revision history
- 2026-03-10 created quick list
- 2026-03-12 added platform notes + PR blurb
- 2026-03-13 expanded streaming examples + schedule

source.title: cross-cohort planning scratch
source.body:
Context: quick 30–45m sync to get Product + Eng + Applied ML aligned on Q2 cross-cohort priorities. This is my scratchpad for prep and the live notes I'll use during the meeting. Informal — lots of open questions.

Primary goal (what we must leave with): a clear prioritized list of 3 deliverables we can put on the shared roadmap board + owners and gating criteria for each. Secondary: rough capacity/cost envelope and a measurable quality signal for rollout.

Meeting logistics:
- Attendees (asked): Priya (PM), Omar (Eng lead, serving runtime), Sofia (Applied ML), Maya (Product Ops)
- Timebox: 30m (first 20m decisions, last 10m actions + blockers)
- Pre-reads: Q2 goals doc (Confluence link above), RINF-2412 (model-variant matrix), PR #178 (canary infra)

Agenda (compact)
1) 0–5m: Quick context & success metrics (Priya)
2) 5–15m: Proposals from Eng + ML — tradeoffs and blockers (Omar & Sofia)
3) 15–22m: Decide top 3 cross-cohort deliverables + owners
4) 22–30m: Action items, risks, next check-in cadence

Things I want to call out up front (notes to self):
- Keep scope to what impacts hosted API + dedicated first; private deployments can be follow-up unless security gating is required.
- Emphasize measurable SLOs (p50/p95 latency, cost per 1k tokens, quality regressions by eval set).
- Avoid getting lost in micro-ops (exact autoscaling config) — capture as follow-ups.

Proposed candidate deliverables (brainstorm):
A) Model-variant rollout policy: finalize mapping (fast/cheap/accurate) -> hosted/dedicated/private. Goal: reduce average token cost by 15% without >2pt drop on key evals. Owner: Sofia (ML) + Omar (Eng). Gating: validation evals pass, perf profile validated on 2 regions. Ticket: JIRA-RINF-2412.
B) Canary + fallback automation: automated canary runs with automatic fallback to baseline model on regression or perf breach. Owner: Omar. Gating: PR #178 merged + 2 canary runs in prod-mock.
C) Prefix/KV cache tuning + suggested batching config: achieve latency SLOs for 75th pct of requests while enabling batching for low-concurrency flows. Owner: Eng infra (TBD) + Sofia for correctness. Gating: internal perf test showing 10–20% cost delta, no regression on tail latency.
D) Eval harness + monthly quality monitor: curated prompt set and automated regression alerts. Owner: Priya + Sofia. Gating: alerts wired into Console, sample review workflow.

Prioritization rationale (quick):
- A (variant policy) touches cost + dev UX — high impact, medium effort.
- B (canary automation) critical for reliability, lower scope if we re-use existing tooling.
- C (cache/batching) likely higher engineering effort; big upside on cost but risky for latency tails.
- D (eval harness) relatively low engineering but needs ML curation; critical to detect regressions early.

Open questions to surface in meeting:
- Priya: which customer segments push for private deployment support in Q2? any contracts mandating feature X?
- Sofia: do we need to freeze model weights for canary? or allow quantized variants to be swapped dynamically?
- Omar: capacity envelope — do we have headroom on Dedicated pool to run canaries without affecting customers? exact numbers?
- Ops: who owns rollback playbook steps? is there an on-call runbook?

Quick cost/capacity sketch (back-of-envelope):
- Baseline: hosted API 600k tokens/day across 3 regions, avg cost currently $0.002/token (example).
- Target after variant policy + quantization: 15% lower unit cost -> moves to $0.0017/token.
- Canary overhead: need ~5–10% capacity for safe canaries (estimate).
- Action: ask infra for Dedicated pool headroom numbers — Omar to provide by 2026-03-18.

Risks and mitigations:
- Risk: quality regression post-quant -> Mitigation: gated evals + canary traffic split + rollback automation.
- Risk: increased tail latency due to batching -> Mitigation: latency SLO + per-route config to disable batching for latency-sensitive routes.
- Risk: cross-team misalignment on owners -> Mitigation: list owners now, confirm by end of week, escalate to Priya if unconfirmed.

Decisions I expect to get in this meeting:
- Top 3 deliverables to commit to for initial roadmap tile.
- Owner nominations for each deliverable.
- A hard gating checklist for canary -> staged rollout.

Proposed quick action items (assignments below are my suggested defaults):
- Omar: confirm Dedicated pool headroom + rough QPS numbers (due 2026-03-18).
- Sofia: finalize candidate eval set (50 prompts) for variant policy tests (due 2026-03-20).
- Priya: update Confluence Q2 plan with selected top-3 and owners (due 2026-03-16).
- Lucas (me): synthesize notes from this meeting, create follow-up checklist + add to shared roadmap board (due 2026-03-14).
- Maya: draft rollout communication plan template for customers (internal + public blog skeleton) (due 2026-03-22).

Live notes placeholder (will paste meeting minutes here):
- [00:00] Priya: framing — focus on cost + reliability tradeoffs, customers ask for predictable latency.
- [05:10] Omar: capacity: Dedicated has ~25% headroom, but region eu-west is tight; canaries should run in us-east first.
- [07:30] Sofia: variant test shows top-1 accuracy drops 1.8 pts on our eval; quantization saq reduces memory but increases perplexity slightly.
- [12:00] Decision: pick A (variant policy), B (canary infra), D (eval harness) as the top-3 for initial cycle. C deprioritized to follow-up.
- [20:00] Action assigned — see list above.

Follow-ups (post-meeting):
- Create shared board card for each deliverable with owner, JIRA link, acceptance criteria. (Lucas)
- Omar to schedule infra deep-dive w/ infra team to scope C if we want to revive it (Omar).
- Sofia to run targeted eval runs comparing baseline vs quantized variant and post results to PR #178 thread.

Notes-to-self (personal scratch):
- Keep faith in small scope: three deliverables is enough for this cycle.
- Watch for product requests that expand scope (privates + HIPAA) — ask Priya to flag any active deals.
- Remind Priya to include metrics owners (who will own monthly quality reports).

Unresolved during meeting (capture):
- exact definition of "cost per 1k tokens" for mixed requests (how to normalize multi-turn chat). Need metric spec. (Action: Priya + Analytics)
- fallback latency target threshold for automatic rollback (suggest p95 > 1.5x baseline) — confirm.

Reference snippets I might paste into Confluence/board:
- "Top-3 Q2 cross-cohort commitments: model-variant policy (owner Sofia+Omar), canary+fallback automation (owner Omar), eval harness + monthly monitor (owner Priya+Sofia). Acceptance: gated evals + canary validated in us-east."

-- end scratch --

(Will update body after meeting and then move to shared drive parent doc once polished.)

source.title: Coffee-kernel minimalist log — Arpit
source.content:
Quick context: caffeine-fueled catch-all for today. Focus: short repro of late-morning p99 spike on small prompts, quick triage steps, minimal TODOs so I can sprint on the microbatch scheduler fix after standup.

Summary (TL;DR):
- P99 up ~350ms on hosted-api route /v1/generate for 32-token prompts between 09:30–10:15 PT. Baseline normally ~120ms.
- Suspect: KV cache miss storm combined with aggressive microbatch window increase after autoscaler burst.
- Repro steps below; quick mitigation = revert microbatch-window to 6ms, re-enable per-model prefix cache TTL.

Observed signals:
- Grafana quick check: route gen-hosted-usw2 spike correlates with instance scale-up events (autoscale + burst). CPU fine, but GPU SM util drops to ~40% at spike start, then recovers.
- Logs: many WARNs: "kv-cache: miss_rate 0.92" during window.
- Tracing: requests that hit p99 show cache lookup + redis RTT + de-dup path taking ~180ms, then enqueue wait another ~120ms.

Quick repro (local-ish):
1) Checkout branch: runtime@981 (PR linked above).
2) Start local dev stack with a small redis (redis-server --port 6379) and MODEL=redwood-14b-quant dev config.
3) run: ./dev/bench/send-bursts.sh --concurrency 80 --qps 150 --seq-len 32 --duration 900
4) watch `tail -f logs/runtime.log | grep "kv-cache"` — you should see consecutive miss warnings and queue depth rising.

Hypothesis: when autoscaler adds nodes, routing briefly shards active prefixes across new backends; prefix cache placements invalidated -> simultaneous cache population attempts -> cache stampede + microbatch window expands to include more requests (policy: adaptive-window grows when enqueue depth high) -> increased latency while caches warm.

Immediate mitigation (safe):
- [ ] Revert adaptive microbatch growth threshold from 300->600 tokens for hosted tier (PR draft: revert-microbatch-growth).
- [ ] Re-enable per-model prefix TTL (short-term: 10s -> 60s) to reduce stampede on scale events.
- [ ] Add a simple jitter to cache-populate path to randomize re-pop attempts.

Work items (priority order):
1) Short patch: add jitter + single-flight guard around prefix-populate (estimate 2–3h). Owner: Arpit.
2) Medium: change autoscaler routing to pin hot prefixes for 30s after scale event (safety window). Owner: Mina.
3) Long: telemetry to surface cache-stampede early (trace-based alerting + k8s-event correlation). Owner: Ethan.

Notes to self (commands / quick snippets):
- Check redis stats: redis-cli INFO persistence | egrep 'used_memory_human|connected_clients'
- Grep logs: rg "kv-cache.*miss|enqueue-depth" -n --hidden logs/ | sed -n '1,200p'

title: Coffee-kernel minimalist log — Arpit
preview:
Quick context: caffeine-fueled catch-all for today. Focus: short repro of late-morning p99 spike on small prompts, quick triage steps, minimal TODOs so I can sprint on the microbatch scheduler fix after standup.

Summary (TL;DR):
- P99 up ~350ms on hosted-api route /v1/generate for 32-token prompts between 09:30–10:15 PT. Baseline normally ~120ms.
- Suspect: KV cache miss storm combined with aggressive microbatch window increase after autoscaler burst.
- Repro steps below; quick mitigation = revert microbatch-window to 6ms, re-enable per-model prefix cache TTL.

Observed signals:
- Grafana quick check: route gen-hosted-usw2 spike correlates with instance scale-up events (autoscale + burst). CPU fine, but GPU SM util drops to ~40% at spike start, then recovers.
- Logs: many WARNs: "kv-cache: miss_rate 0.92" during window.
- Tracing: requests that hit p99 show cache lookup + redis RTT + de-dup path taking ~180ms, then enqueue wait another ~120ms.

Quick repro (local-ish):
1) Checkout branch: runtime@981 (PR linked above).
2) Start local dev stack with a small redis (redis-server --port 6379) and MODEL=redwood-14b-quant dev config.
3) run: ./dev/bench/send-bursts.sh --concurrency 80 --qps 150 --seq-len 32 --duration 900
4) watch `tail -f logs/runtime.log | grep "kv-cache"` — you should see consecutive miss warnings and queue depth rising.

Hypothesis: when autoscaler adds nodes, routing briefly shards active prefixes across new backends; prefix cache placements invalidated -> simultaneous cache population attempts -> cache stampede + microbatch window expands to include more requests (policy: adaptive-window grows when enqueue depth high) -> increased latency while caches warm.

Immediate mitigation (safe):
- [ ] Revert adaptive microbatch growth threshold from 300->600 tokens for hosted tier (PR draft: revert-microbatch-growth).
- [ ] Re-enable per-model prefix TTL (short-term: 10s -> 60s) to reduce stampede on scale events.
- [ ] Add a simple jitter to cache-populate path to randomize re-pop attempts.

Work items (priority order):
1) Short patch: add jitter + single-flight guard around prefix-populate (estimate 2–3h). Owner: Arpit.
2) Medium: change autoscaler routing to pin hot prefixes for 30s after scale event (safety window). Owner: Mina.
3) Long: telemetry to surface cache-stampede early (trace-based alerting + k8s-event correlation). Owner: Ethan.

Notes to self (commands / quick snippets):
- Check redis stats: redis-cli INFO persistence | egrep 'used_memory_human|connected_clients'
- Grep logs: rg "kv-cache.*miss|enqueue-depth" -n --hidden logs/ | sed -n '1,200p'
- Single-flight sketch (golang): sync.Map + channel per-prefix / select with timeout 200ms

- Mina to prototype routing pin for hot prefixes.
- Ethan to add trace filter to surface cache miss storms.

Open questions / follow-ups:
- Are there other places where prefix population is duplicated (e.g., model-variant fallback path)? Check fallback chain in call graph; add tests.
- Should we implement a grace period after scale-up where microbatch window is frozen? Pros/cons — discuss with SRE.

source.title: Marketplace integration precall cheatsheet — AWS/Azure/GCP partners
source.body:
Context:
- Prep for tranche of partner syncs the week of 2026-03-15. Quick cadence: 20–30m precall, then 45m partner session. This doc is my personal cheat sheet for the precall + immediate follow-ups.
- Partners: AWS Marketplace (partner success lead: R. Alvarez), Azure Marketplace (technical PM: L. Kaur), GCP Marketplace (channel rep: B. Singh).
- Goal: align on integration blockers, enrollment status, pricing/offer language, and next-step timeline to push listing to marketplace within 6–8 weeks.

Agenda (20m precall):
1) Quick status round (2m) — ask them to state the single biggest blocker.
2) Demo/tech hooks summary (5m) — what we need from their side (entitlement, SaaS contract link, deployment URL).
3) Commercials check (5m) — list price, BYOC/bring-your-own-license policy, committed usage (if any).
4) Operational items & compliance (4m) — KMS/HSM key exchange, audit log requirements, SOC2 or ISO docs.
5) Next steps & owners (3m) — confirm who will do what this week.

Quick openers / ice-breaker lines:
- "Love what your team shipped on the partner console — few follow-up questions so we can push to public listing"
- "I want to make sure the technical checklist is fully green before the larger integration call so we don't waste time."

Partner-specific prep notes:
- AWS:
  - Entitlement model options: SaaS Subscriptions vs AMI/Bring your own infra. Ask whether they prefer consolidated billing or subscription token exchange.
  - AWS Legal usually asks for explicit data residency language — confirm if customer-facing copy ok or need AWS-approved wording.
  - Need marketplace product code (we have draft), confirm contact for POC account provisioning.
  - Risk: AWS sometimes delays for contract signatures if BYOL. Flag to legal if needed.

- Azure:
  - Licensing: Azure prefers transact/bring-your-own both supported; verify which SKUs they will prefer.
  - Technical: check if they require Azure AD SSO SAML metadata or SCIM for provisioning — we support OIDC + SCIM. Prep sample SCIM endpoint notes.
  - Compliance: Azure marketplace listing requires specific logo sizes and partner validation — confirm MPN ID.

- GCP:
  - GCP likes containerized deployments; confirm whether they want marketplace SaaS (routing through GCP) or listing that points to our hosted endpoint.
  - Ask about data export requirements (GCP customers often ask about BigQuery connectors) — we don't have an out-of-box connector, but we can propose an integration play.

Technical checklist (things I need to confirm or have in hand):
- Product SKU / product code for each marketplace (AWS product code, Azure offer id, GCP listing id).
- Example SaaS contract / terms URL for linking on listing.
- Sample entitlement token exchange flow (JWT/manual header) with expiration TTL.
- Demo account + test entitlements created before partner session.
- Compliance artifacts: SOC2 Type II cert (yes), pen test summary (partial), privacy policy link.

Commercial & GTM messaging notes:
- Pricing templates: list monthly unit price + per-seat vs per-token options. For marketplace listing we should present list price and indicate available discounts for committed usage.
- Preferred ask this round: secure "trial listing" with GA price TBD, push for 12-week pilot commit if customer asks for payment terms.
- Narrow ask for partner: "Can we agree on a pilot contract (60 days) with a sandbox customer, and a go/no-go decision at end of pilot?"

Open questions to surface during call:
- Do they require a region-specific hosting pledge (e.g., EU-only) for initial customers? If yes, who signs off internally?
- Are there specific marketing co-funding expectations for co-marketing at launch? (ask % or budget).
- Which internal partner developer should we loop in for entitlement debugging?

Canned responses (short):
- If asked about latency SLA: "We provide latency profiles per model and can commit to a P99 SLA for dedicated capacity; for hosted listings we'll include expected response times on the listing."
- If asked about data retention: "We can configure customer-level retention policies and offer private deployment for regulated customers via Redwood Private."

Notes to self (scratchpad):
- Ask Maya to confirm whether our test entitlements support multiple concurrent tokens (we had a bug last quarter).
- Samir to follow up on SOC2 evidence: need cert.pdf v2.1 from security.
- Draft sample legal clause for BYOL flow — include templates from confluence page.

Follow-up action items (capture during call, fill owner/due after):
- [ ] Send AWS product code template and sample entitlement flow to Alvarez (owner: Ethan) — due: 2026-03-18
- [ ] Confirm Azure MPN ID and SCIM metadata with L. Kaur (owner: Maya) — due: 2026-03-17
- [ ] Upload SOC2 Type II docs to shared folder and share link with partner (owner: Samir) — due: 2026-03-16
- [ ] Create demo tenant + test tokens for partner testing (owner: DevRel team, contact: Priya) — due: 2026-03-20
- [ ] Draft pilot offer language for marketplace listing (owner: Ethan + GTM) — due: 2026-03-19

Follow-up email template (quick):
Subject: Next steps after our marketplace precall
Hi <PartnerName>,
Thanks for the quick sync — as discussed: attaching the product code template, entitlement flow example, and our SOC2 cert. Proposed next steps: 1) demo tenant provisioning (week of 3/22), 2) pilot contract signed (60d), 3) marketing checklist and launch date by 4/30.
I'll follow up with owners for each item.
Best,
Ethan

Risks & escalations (flag to AE/Revenue/Legal):
- If partner requires lengthy legal review for BYOL language, escalate to Legal — potential 4–6 week delay.
- If partner insists on isolated EU-only hosting for first 6 months, ops implications (capacity planning) — loop infra.

Checklist before larger partner integration call (what I must have ready):
- Demo tenant with 3 test users + entitlement tokens (green)
- One pager showing expected cost per 100k tokens (simple table)
- Draft pilot contract (legal reviewed)
- Shared folder with compliance artifacts and marketing logos

Random thoughts / brainstorming (keepsakes):
- Could we propose a marketplace-native trial where license is auto-activated via token exchange — frictionless onboarding? Build as a small feature to reduce dropout.
- Consider a short checklist on our listing page: "Supported integrations: OIDC, SCIM, KMS" — quick wins for discoverability.

Bottomline for call: come across helpful, reduce friction, and push a single next concrete ask (pilot agreement or demo provisioning). Keep the ask small so partner engagement momentum survives internal reviews.

Bottomline for call: come across helpful, reduce friction, and push a single next concrete ask (pilot agreement or demo provisioning). Keep the ask small so partner engagement momentum survives internal reviews.

source.title: Tempo Bridge — Weekly Serving/Infra/Console Kickoff (prep scratch)
source.content:
Purpose / framing:
- Quick sync for engineering folks touching Serving Runtime, Infra, and Console. Keep it short (30–40m) and walk out with 3-4 concrete action items.
- Primary goals: unblock rollouts, confirm next-step owners for the autoscaling pilot, surface any console UX regressions tied to recent runtime changes.

Meeting logistics (Tues 09:30 PT):
- Host: Dante Morales (me) — will start the recording and tldr at the top
- Regular cadence: weekly (Tues), 30–40m, optional deep-dive follow-ups scheduled ad-hoc
- Attendees (invite list): eng-serving-runtime, infra on-call (Liam), console PM (Anika), product owner (Priya)

Pre-reads (short):
- Autoscale pilot doc (one-pager) — link in the Confluence runbook
- Last week’s rollout notes: INF-421 (Jira)
- Console errors snapshot (past 72h) — pasted below

Agenda (timeboxed):
1) 09:30–09:35 (5m) — Quick round: anything blocking you (single-sentence callouts)
2) 09:35–09:45 (10m) — Autoscaling pilot status and immediate risks (Liam + Dante)
   - Key question: do we enable burst capacity for Prod Canary?
3) 09:45–09:55 (10m) — Console regressions: observed errors, user-reported flows, and mitigation options (Anika)
   - Focus: prompt editing + live streaming errors; are these P0/P1?
4) 09:55–10:00 (5m) — Rollouts & fallback policy check (Priya)
   - Confirm canary % and automatic rollback thresholds
5) 10:00–10:10 (10m) — Action items recap and short blockers list; schedule follow-ups as needed

Quick notes / observations (scratch):
- Console: two persistent JS stack traces tied to streaming client when token limit reached — reproducible on staging, intermittent on prod.
- Runtime: kv-cache eviction appears spike-y during the E2E loadtest last Friday; suspect batch window misconfiguration (batch size vs latency SLO).
- Infra: instance churn due to healthcheck flapping on instance start; logs show a race with the readiness probe (500ms timeout).

Action item tracker (live, update after meeting):
- AI-01: Investigate KV-cache eviction pattern and propose safe default batch window tweak (owner: Dante) — due: 2026-03-17 — status: in_progress
- AI-02: Add defensive guard in console streaming client to handle early EOF/token cap gracefully (owner: Anika) — due: 2026-03-15 — status: open
- AI-03: Tune readiness probe backoff and add jitter to reduce instance flapping (owner: Liam) — due: 2026-03-16 — status: open
- AI-04: Define canary rollout threshold (percent + error budget) and document in runbook (owner: Priya) — due: 2026-03-13 — status: in_review

Parking lot / follow-ups (schedule 30m deep dives if needed):
- Deep dive: batching heuristics vs variable sequence lengths — invite Research if we change kernel selection logic
- Postmortem ask: assemble timeline for last Friday’s E2E test (logs + tracing) — decide if retro required

Questions I want to surface in 1:1s this week (for manager / tech lead):
- Is there appetite to promote the autoscale pilot to dedicated-reserved pool for a weekend smoke test? Pros/cons and financial volatility?
- Headcount ask: do we prioritize one infra hiring for long-tail flakiness vs two small infra sprints?

Notes to self (informal):
- Keep the kickoff under 40m — if tangents grow, call a separate deep-dive and assign a time immediately.
- When calling out errors, always include repro steps for console folks.
- Bring trace IDs to the meeting (copy into chat) — saves 5–10 mins.

Staging error snapshot (paste):
- 2026-03-09T02:14:11Z streaming-client.js: Uncaught (in promise) StreamClosedError: early EOF
- 2026-03-09T02:14:12Z runtime: eviction delta=12 MB in 7s, window=300ms
- 2026-03-09T08:50:23Z infra: startup healthcheck failed (timeout) instance-id i-0b7a3d

Quick checklist before kickoff (Dante):
- [ ] Post agenda in #eng-serving w/ TL;DR
- [ ] Attach canary metric graph to calendar invite
- [ ] Add action-item table template to the doc for live editing
- [ ] Ensure Anika has staging repro steps in chat

End of scratch. Keep this doc as the canonical live notes for the tempo-bridge weekly; I'll move to Confluence if it stabilizes.

source.title: Prelaunch operator daybook
source.content:
Purpose: quick solo-run notes for release cutover. Lightweight, choppiest — my live checklist and reminders. Not the canonical runbook; pared-down for single-operator day run.

Status: draft. Will handoff to oncall if I need to step away. Add any final backports here and blast to #releases.

SUMMARY TIMELINE (my target)
- T-90m: wake + coffee, brief the oncall (if oncall handed over), verify staging smoke green
- T-45m: merge remaining small backports, kick CI for canary image build, start Canary deployment window
- T-20m: smoke test canary, sanity check telemetry, open comms post templates
- T-0m: traffic switch (20% -> 100% over 5min), monitor P99/P95, CPU/GPU util, queue depth
- +30m: confirm rollout healthy, tidy PRs, update postmortem stub if any anomalies

QUICK CHECKS (preflight)
- auth: ensure keyrotation job completed (last run: 2026-03-09 23:10 UTC)
- certs: edge TLS certs show valid in cert dashboard (no expiry within 30 days)
- infra: node-pools autoscaler config unchanged since last release? (run: gcloud container node-pools describe)
- perf baseline: p95 latency for /v1/generate <= 210ms under canary profile (use viz link below)

BACKPORT / CHERRY-PICK NOTES (todo / PRs to merge)
- REL-4821: backport fix for kv-cache eviction off-by-one — PR-9987 (merge to release-2026.03)
- REL-4817: small audit-log metadata rename — PR-9999 (needs rebase; small conflict in schema.json)
- quick & dirty: add healthcheck timeout increase (PR-10001) for host-gateway when downstream slow

COMMANDS I RUN OFTEN (put these in terminal)
- build canary image: ./tools/build_image.sh --tag release-2026.03-canary
- deploy canary: kubectl apply -f k8s/canary-deployment.yaml -n redwood-prod-canary
- check pods: kubectl get pods -n redwood-prod-canary -o wide
- stream logs (gateway): kubectl logs -f svc/gateway -n redwood-prod-canary --since=10m
- curl smoke: curl -sS -X POST https://canary.api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -d '{"input":"smoke test"}' --header 'Authorization: Bearer $SMOKE_KEY' -v

SMOKE TEST LIST (minimal to pass)
- auth token accepted, 200 OK
- token-level billing ID in response metadata present
- truncated generation length consistent with prompt
- streaming chunk ordering preserved (quick eyeball)

METRICS / DASHBOARDS TO WATCH (open these before T-20)
- Canary dashboard: https://dash.internal/redwood/canary-release-2026-03
- Latency: p50/p95/p99 for inference route (/v1/generate)
- Errors: 5xx rate, model-serving timeouts, queue retries
- Cost signal: tokens/sec and GPU utilization (watch sudden spikes)

ALERTS / ESCALATION TRIGGERS (my thresholds)
- >1% 5xx sustained for 5min -> pause rollout and investigate
- P95 > 500ms for 3 consecutive 1-min buckets -> rollback to previous model variant
- GPU OOM events -> scale down rollout, escalate to infra (Roberto)

ROLLBACK PLAN (fast)
- Abort canary scaling: kubectl scale deployment/gateway --replicas=0 -n redwood-prod-canary
- Revert traffic in router: switch route config to release-2026.02 (hash a1b2c3)
- Trigger emergency: #releases: "Rolling back release-2026.03 due to XX" (use template below)

COMMS TEMPLATES (copy-paste)
- Canary OK (T-20): "Canary for release-2026.03 deployed. Running smoke checks. Will update at T-0. Owner: @EthanP"
- Rollout start (T-0): "Starting rollout -> release-2026.03. Targeting 100% traffic in 5m. Monitor: p95 latency, errors. Contact: @EthanP, @oncall-sre"
- Rollback notice: "Rolling back release-2026.03 to release-2026.02 due to elevated 5xx. Investigating root cause. Oncall: @EthanP"

NOTES TO SELF / REMINDERS (messy)
- If schema conflict in REL-4817, prioritize metadata surface (analytics) — patch can wait until post-cutover if blocking
- Keep an eye on redis swap; last time we saw queue backpressure when batch size increased
- If need to step away, ping Jan 11:30 (Jana) and handoff log link here: https://notes.local/handovers/2026-03-10

POST-RUN TODOS (after +60m)
- tidy PRs and tag release notes
- open short incident stub if anything >5% errors
- update canary settings to default once stable

OPEN QUESTIONS / TODO (short)
- Should we bump kv-cache TTL in this release or defer? (pros: fewer cold-cache misses; cons: larger mem footprint)
- Confirm final canary traffic ramp plan with product (was 20% -> 100% in 5m ok?)

ENDPOINTS & CONTACTS
- Ops pager: +1-555-0110 (use only after Slack triage)
- Infra owner: Roberto Gomez
- Release owner (product): Maya Thompson

Last manual note: breathe. Keep the checklist tight and do not multitask while the ramp is active. If in doubt, pause the rollout and ask for a second set of eyes.

-- EP

source.title: All-hands framing playbook — leadership update (scratch)
source.content:
Purpose / quick note:
- Draft scratchpad for CEO/CTO/Head of Product talking points and expected Qs. Not final. Use as facilitator crib during rehearsal.

High-level goals (what we want people to leave with):
- Clear rationale for the change (why now) — tie to customer signals and runway/scale needs.
- Confidence in continuity (who owns what, who to go to).
- Realistic about disruption — acknowledge uncertainty, but show plan & guardrails.
- Concrete next steps + how org will be supported (hiring, budgets, resources).

Audience breakdown:
- ICs (primary): want clarity, day-to-day impact, reporting lines.
- Managers: need escalation lanes, clarity on headcount, performance expectations.
- Cross-functional partners: how collaboration shifts, product priorities.

Top-line announcement framing (lead-in):
- 30–45s headline: what changed, who’s stepping into what role, immediate effect (none/minor), timeline for operational changes.
- One-sentence why: tie to product momentum + need for deliberate structure to scale reliably. Avoid vague corporate cliches.

Suggested opening script for CEO (approx 2–3 min):
- Thank everyone, acknowledge intensity of past quarter.
- Say the basic fact: reorganizing leadership to match scale stage — name the leaders and new responsibilities.
- Emphasize continuity of product mission and values.
- Call out what will not change (support, billing, customer commitments).
- Close with empathy + invite questions in Q&A and follow-up office hours.

CTO/Head of Product micro-points (30–90s):
- Technical priorities unchanged: latency, reliability, and rollout hygiene.
- Dedicated: we will keep momentum on Redwood Dedicated roadmap; private deployments remain top priority for enterprise.
- Engineering ops: immediate reporting matrix (link to org chart draft) and 2-week transition plan.

Anticipated Qs + crib answers (short):
- Q: "Will this impact my role/headcount?"
  A: "No immediate mass changes. We’re aligning leadership and will share team-level plans within 10 business days; individual managers will connect 1:1s"
- Q: "Who do I ask about project roadmaps?"
  A: "Product leads remain primary; they’ll publish updated roadmap syncs and link to Confluence space."
- Q: "Why now?"
  A: "Model maturity + scale inflection — we need clearer ownership for hosted vs private product lines to move faster and reduce operational risk."
- Q: "How will performance be evaluated?"
  A: "Same core metrics + clearer SLAs for critical systems; managers will review KPIs in next cycle."
- Q: "What about hiring/backfills?"
  A: "We’re prioritizing roles tied to availability and infra stability; people ops will publish a hiring cadence doc."

Open/Hard questions to surface to founders before finalizing:
- How explicit should we be about budget constraints vs growth narrative? (risk: demoralize if too blunt)
- Do we commit to any immediate org-wide freezes/hires?
- Phrase around promotions/transfers that could be sensitive — need legal check?

Logistics & facilitation notes:
- Run rehearsal with CEO + CTO + two managers (Ben, Lina) on Thurs 3/12 4pm PT.
- Q&A moderator: Priya (collects live Qs + sorts for follow-ups).
- Record session. Post-ops: all-hands recording + FAQ doc published within 24h.
- Set up two office-hour slots (24h and 72h after announcement).

Slide placeholders (minimal):
1) Headline slide: single-sentence change + org snapshot (visual).
2) Rationale slide: bullets (customer signals, product scale, speed to market).
3) Who’s who + responsibilities (org chart).
4) Timeline & next steps (what happens in 2w/30d/90d).
5) FAQ + how to get help.

Follow-ups / TODOs (ownership + eta):
- Draft all-hands announcement email (Anika) — 3/12 EOD.
- Publish initial FAQ stub (Ben) — within 24h after meeting.
- Org chart update (Lina) — 3/13 morning.
- 1:1 manager guide template (People Ops) — 3/14.

Tone notes: keep concise, transparent, empathetic. Avoid absolutes ("we will") when uncertain — prefer "we intend to" or "we plan to". Use examples for critical points (customer incidents, latency SLOs) where it helps clarity.

Loose ideas / brain dump (can prune):
- Small-group office hours by function (infra, product, CS) to reduce noise.
- Dedicated thread for anonymous questions (Google form) to surface concerns.
- Consider FAQ "If this affects your career path" section with concrete paths.
- Follow-up pulse survey 2 weeks post to measure sentiment + questions.

Notes to self: keep this doc lightweight. Convert to formal comms email + Confluence FAQ after rehearsal. Mark changes with initials and date.

END scratch — do not share externally

source.title: Asymmetric risks and asks — exec sync scratchpad
source.body:
Purpose / framing:
- Quick prep notes for Friday exec staff sync (30m).
- Focus: surface asymmetric risks (low-probability, high-impact) + 3 pragmatic asks we need a decision on before next Tuesday.
- Keep it terse; bring only what requires cross-functional blocking decisions.

Meeting script (30m):
- 0:00-02:00: opening context (me) — why asymmetric risks matter this week (short)
- 02:00-10:00: Top 3 asymmetric risks (1–3 minute read + 2–3 questions each)
- 10:00-20:00: Asks / decision items (1–2 min pitch each, commit owners and fallback)
- 20:00-27:00: Quick cross-team clarifying Qs (SRE, Finance, Legal)
- 27:00-30:00: Commitments, who owns follow-ups, and parking lot capture

Context notes (to self):
- Signals: spike in tail latency on model A in us-west last 72h — transient but correlates with nightly backups on infra pool 3.
- Finance: unexpected spend tier bump projected if we leave burst autoscaling unbounded for Dedicated customers this quarter.
- Sales: two enterprise trials ask for on-prem proof-of-concept; legal wants threat model before committing.

Top 3 asymmetric risks (brief props, impact, mitigation options):
1) Model-serving cascade during nightly infra ops
   - Why it’s asymmetric: small maintenance event -> disproportionate global latency/regression for synchronous customers.
   - Recent evidence: 3 incidents in 10 days, median P95 increased by 240ms for affected accounts; one support case escalated to AM.
   - Potential impact: churn in top 10 paying customers if repeats; SLA hit.
   - Options: (A) windowed maintenance + automatic traffic drain to warm pools (requires engineering work + 2-week runway); (B) stricter change freeze during critical windows (policy + ops discipline); (C) temporary throttle on scheduled backups during business hours.
   - Ask: prioritize (A) or (B) this week? 
   - Estimate: (A) ~3 FTE-weeks infra/serving-runtime + config rollout; (B) org policy communication (negligible infra work).

2) Cost cliff from unbounded Dedicated burst usage
   - Why asymmetric: one or two large Dedicated customers can push us into a higher spend bracket quickly; negotiation window is small.
   - Evidence: usage telemetry shows 2 Dedicated customers are trending +60% above committed baseline due to weekend batch jobs.
   - Potential impact: quarterly margin erosion, triggers for finance to pause new hires in Q2.
   - Options: (A) enforce burst caps and notify customers with remediation plan; (B) offer quick temp uplift (short-term revenue, long-term risk); (C) instrument billing alerts and pre-authorization flows.
   - Ask: approve temporary uplift offer (revenue > cost) for customer X with a 30-day review, or enforce cap now?
   - Estimate: legal/ops review ~3 days; product engineering ~1 week if we add pre-auth path.

3) Compliance gating for first on-prem pilot
   - Why asymmetric: failure to provide a clear threat model/audit scope could delay pilot >6 weeks.
   - Evidence: customer legal asked for HSM/KMS mapping and data flow diagrams; security backlog currently has two high-priority audits.
   - Potential impact: delays the sale and distracts applied-ML + infra teams.
   - Options: (A) prioritize a small scoped threat model deliverable for pilot (security + solutions eng pod for 5 days); (B) negotiate delayed delivery with customer in exchange for reduced scope pilot; (C) shift pilot to Dedicated as interim.
   - Ask: green-light (A) or choose (B) with product/sales to push timeline?
   - Estimate: (A) ~1.5 FTE-week overall (security + solutions eng).

Decision matrix proposals (one-liners to present):
- Risk 1: implement traffic-drain feature (A) — higher engineering cost, reduces recurrence risk strongly.
- Risk 2: offer temporary uplift with contractual guardrails — balances revenue vs marginal cost.
- Risk 3: prioritize threat-model deliverable for pilot (A) to protect the sales window.

Three concrete asks (for the Execs to commit to during sync):
1) Ops/Infra: Approve redirecting two serving-engineering sprints (3 FTE-weeks) to implement traffic-drain and warm-pool readiness by April 1. Owner: Priya (SRE lead). Fallback: policy freeze.
2) Finance/Sales: Approve one-time temporary uplift for Dedicated customer X for 30 days with specific usage caps and invoice adjustments; require legal sign-off via 48-hour expedited review. Owner: Leo (sales lead) + Maria (Finance). Fallback: enforce current burst caps and send remediation notice.
3) Security/Legal: Allocate security bandwidth for a scoped threat-model (deliverable: 3-page dataflow + HSM/KMS appendix) to unblock on-prem pilot within 10 business days. Owner: Sophie (security). Fallback: route pilot to Dedicated with clear constraints.

Pre-reads to attach (brief):
- tail-latency-incident-log-3d (internal link) — includes trace snippets and hostmaps
- dedicated-usage-rollup-march (sheet) — top dedicated accounts + trend lines
- legal-onprem-checklist (confluence) — customer asks list and current gaps

Notes-to-self / speaker cues:
- Start with a one-sentence consequence: "If we don’t act, we risk losing 2 enterprise customers and a 0.5% margin hit this quarter." Keep tone factual, not alarmist.
- Use the 5-minute rule: if a decision can be deferred without ramping risk, park it to product committee.
- If the room splits, propose a time-boxed pilot (14d) and owner commitment, not open-ended promises.

Open questions to surface:
- Are we comfortable approving temporary uplift for revenue protection without automated billing guardrails? (Finance needs explicit statement.)
- Can Priya commit to the sprints trade-off given her current on-call rotations? (SRE capacity check.)
- If we prioritize security for pilot, what else drops (which audit shifts)? Need explicit trade-offs from security head.

Parking lot (capture items to follow up after sync):
- Deep dive telemetry session on backups vs serving latency (SRE + serving runtime).
- Billing UI quick patch to show usage alerts in console (product/infra).
- Draft customer comms template for burst/cost incidents (CS + comms).

Follow-up template (to send immediately after meeting):
- Subject: Exec sync decisions + owners (date)
- Body: 1) decisions made, 2) owners & deadlines, 3) next checkpoint (date/time), 4) docs linked.

Rough timeline if asks approved:
- Traffic-drain: start 3/16, QA 3/25, rollout 4/01
- Dedicated uplift: immediate if legal sign-off by 3/14, otherwise caps enforced 3/15
- Threat-model deliverable: start 3/12, deliver by 3/25

Micronotes / quick fragments (messy — keep):
- "If we over-communicate caps customers will game it" — nuance for sales talking points.
- Consider a short-term economics dashboard tile: projected marginal cost of bursts per customer.
- Flag for PM: measure customer tolerance for scheduled maintenance (survey).

End of doc.

(Notes to self: convert this into a 1-pager for the meeting — 1 page max; highlight decisions only.)

   - Estimate: legal/ops review ~3 days; product engineering ~1 week if we add pre-auth path.

3) Compliance gating for first on-prem pilot
   - Why asymmetric: failure to provide a clear threat model/audit scope could delay pilot >6 weeks.
   - Evidence: customer legal asked for HSM/KMS mapping and data flow diagrams; security backlog currently has two high-priority audits.
   - Potential impact: delays the sale and distracts applied-ML + infra teams.
   - Options: (A) prioritize a small scoped threat model deliverable for pilot (security + solutions eng pod for 5 days); (B) negotiate delayed delivery with customer in exchange for reduced scope pilot; (C) shift pilot to Dedicated as interim.
   - Ask: green-light (A) or choose (B) with product/sales to push timeline?
   - Estimate: (A) ~1.5 FTE-week overall (security + solutions eng).

Decision matrix proposals (one-liners to present):
- Risk 1: implement traffic-drain feature (A) — higher engineering cost, reduces recurrence risk strongly.
- Risk 2: offer temporary uplift with contractual guardrails — balances revenue vs marginal cost.
- Risk 3: prioritize threat-model deliverable for pilot (A) to protect the sales window.

Three concrete asks (for the Execs to commit to during sync):
1) Ops/Infra: Approve redirecting two serving-engineering sprints (3 FTE-weeks) to implement traffic-drain and warm-pool readiness by April 1. Owner: Priya (SRE lead). Fallback: policy freeze.
2) Finance/Sales: Approve one-time temporary uplift for Dedicated customer X for 30 days with specific usage caps and invoice adjustments; require legal sign-off via 48-hour expedited review. Owner: Leo (sales lead) + Maria (Finance). Fallback: enforce current burst caps and send remediation notice.
3) Security/Legal: Allocate security bandwidth for a scoped threat-model (deliverable: 3-page dataflow + HSM/KMS appendix) to unblock on-prem pilot within 10 business days. Owner: Sophie (security). Fallback: route pilot to Dedicated with clear constraints.

Pre-reads to attach (brief):
- tail-latency-incident-log-3d (internal link) — includes trace snippets and hostmaps
- dedicated-usage-rollup-march (sheet) — top dedicated accounts + trend lines
- legal-onprem-checklist (confluence) — customer asks list and current gaps

Notes-to-self / speaker cues:
- Start with a one-sentence consequence: "If we don’t act, we risk losing 2 enterprise customers and a 0.5% margin hit this quarter." Keep tone factual, not alarmist.
- Use the 5-minute rule: if a decision can be deferred without ramping risk, park it to product committee.
- If the room splits, propose a time-boxed pilot (14d) and owner commitment, not open-ended promises.

Open questions to surface:
- Are we comfortable approving temporary uplift for revenue protection without automated billing guardrails? (Finance needs explicit statement.)
- Can Priya commit to the sprints trade-off given her current on-call rotations? (SRE capacity check.)
- If we prioritize security for pilot, what else drops (which audit shifts)? Need explicit trade-offs from security head.

Parking lot (capture items to follow up after sync):
- Deep dive telemetry session on backups vs serving latency (SRE + serving runtime).
- Billing UI quick patch to show usage alerts in console (product/infra).
- Draft customer comms template for burst/cost incidents (CS + comms).

Follow-up template (to send immediately after meeting):
- Subject: Exec sync decisions + owners (date)
- Body: 1) decisions made, 2) owners & deadlines, 3) next checkpoint (date/time), 4) docs linked.

Rough timeline if asks approved:
- Traffic-drain: start 3/16, QA 3/25, rollout 4/01
- Dedicated uplift: immediate if legal sign-off by 3/14, otherwise caps enforced 3/15
- Threat-model deliverable: start 3/12, deliver by 3/25

Micronotes / quick fragments (messy — keep):
- "If we over-communicate caps customers will game it" — nuance for sales talking points.
- Consider a short-term economics dashboard tile: projected marginal cost of bursts per customer.
- Flag for PM: measure customer tolerance for scheduled maintenance (survey).

End of doc.

(Notes to self: convert this into a 1-pager for the meeting — 1 page max; highlight decisions only.)

source.title: Prefetch cascade daybook — morning hypotheses
source.content:
Morning quick-capture (rambling, prioritised)

Context
- Noticed another small p99 blip during warmup on small-seq workloads (seq_len ~64) after the recent kv-prefetch changes in the allocator branch. Blips reproducible on one perf rig (rig-07) but not on rig-03.
- Hypothesis: cascade of partial prefetches + numa migration + small-batch packing causes short-lived cache churn that temporarily increases token latency.

Fast checklist / what I ran this morning
1) Reproduce baseline:
   - Checkout: runtime@alloc-prefetch-experiment
   - Run microbench: ./scripts/microbench --model llama-13b --seq 64 --warmup 300 --batch-policy continuous --repeat 6
   - Observed p99 spikes at ~60s and ~220s on rig-07 (cpu/mem lines look normal).
2) Capture traces:
   - sudo perf record -a -g -o /tmp/prefetch-perf.data -- ./runtime-bench ...
   - nvprof not helpful here; used nsight-cu for kernel timing (small jitter seen in memcpy).
3) Heap + KV metrics snapshot (timed):
   - kv_cache.hits=0.72, evictions/s=12, prefetch_requests=384 -> bursts coincide with eviction bursts
   - numa: some threads migrated from node0->node1 at spike time (dmesg shows task affinity bounce)

Hypotheses (ranked)
- H1 (primary): Partial prefetches are generating small asynchronous IOs that align badly with batch completion boundaries; when multiple partials complete together, it triggers a burst of eviction + mutex contention in the KV cache.
- H2: NUMA thread migration due to scheduler decisions interacts with our pinned-thread assumption; occasional migration during prefetch completion causes CPU cache invalidation and delays.
- H3: Small-batch packing changes cause an awkward scheduling pattern where GPU kernels finish concurrently, producing a temporary tail on the CPU side for finalization which we attribute to "network" but is local.

Short experiments to run (today)
- E1 (quick): Add a jittered prefetch-delay in allocator (0..4ms) to spread completions across time, smoke test on rig-07. Metric: p99 drop by >=10% in 20 runs.
- E2: Pin threads more aggressively for warmup path (set CPU affinity for prefetch worker threads). Verify numa migrations via /proc/<pid>/status and psr.
- E3: Instrument KV cache lock hold time around eviction path (+ micro-timers). Build with PERF_TIMER macro and ship to rig-07.
- E4 (control): Revert the small-batch packing tweak locally and repro to validate correlation.

Commands/snippets
- Microbench run I used:
  ./scripts/microbench --model llama-13b --seq 64 --batch 1 --warmup 300 --out=/tmp/microbench-run.json

- Quick perf postprocess:
  perf script -i /tmp/prefetch-perf.data | c++filt | awk '/runtime::prefetch/ {print $0}' | head -n 200 > /tmp/prefetch-top.txt

Observations to note for POST-MORTEM
- Spike windows are short (100–300ms) but align exactly with eviction bursts in the logs.
- Rig-specific: rig-03 behaves fine; compare kernel versions and BIOS power mgmt.
- Prefer short, reversible experiments first (E1, E2) before adding timers/longer instrumentation.

Todo (this file is my short daily todo list)
- [ ] Run E1 on rig-07, record 20 warmup runs (owner: Azra) -- target finish today midday
- [ ] Add temporary jitter flag (runtime --prefetch-jitter-ms) and push to branch
- [ ] Route results to /perf/experiment/prefetch/2026-03-12/ and attach 6 best/worst traces
- [ ] Sync with Lina: quick 15-min check-in after E1 to align next step (collab).
- [ ] If E1 helps, schedule E3 instrument build (Mateo to help with timers).

Notes to self
- Keep changes feature-flagged and small; avoid touching allocator lock hierarchy unless validated.
- Take screenshots of flamegraphs even when spikes are small — they reveal ordering.
- Remember to check power governor on rigs; previous issues came from ondemand governor.

Open questions (brain-dump)
- Could garbage collection in Python shim affect evictions timings? Unlikely at this layer but worth excluding for completeness.
- Any correlation with nightly image build (rig-07 updated kernel last week)? Compare /var/log/apt/history.log.

Next sync
- Quick 10-min standup with Lina @ 13:30 PT to review E1 outcome.
